# Feature: Realtime State Slice

## 1. Feature Goal

**ID**: FEAT-002  
**Source Plan**: `.spec-lite/plan_websocket_sse.md`

Establish protocol-aware, per-tab runtime state for WebSocket and SSE requests inside the core Zustand store. This feature gives later UI and adapter work a stable state contract for connection lifecycle, headers, messages/events, and cleanup behavior, while keeping all realtime data ephemeral and scoped to the active request tab rather than persisted collections.

## 2. Data Model (Granular)

> `.spec-lite/data_model.md` does not exist. This feature defines the concrete store model needed to bridge FEAT-001 request types into tab/runtime state.

### Entities & Attributes

- **TabData** (existing, extended):
  - `id` (string) — Stable tab identifier and the join key for realtime state
  - `protocol` (`RequestProtocol`) — Effective request protocol: `'http' | 'ws' | 'sse'`; no longer a free-form string
  - `method` (string) — Used by HTTP and SSE tabs; ignored when `protocol === 'ws'`
  - `url` (string) — Normalized editor URL string; may use `http://`, `https://`, `ws://`, or `wss://`
  - `params` (`ParamRow[]`) — Request query rows for all protocols
  - `headers` (`HeaderRow[]`) — Request headers for all protocols
  - `body` (`CollectionBody`) — Used by HTTP and SSE tabs; forced to `mode: 'none'` for WS tabs
  - `validation` (`RequestValidation`) — Used only by HTTP tabs; cleared/ignored for WS and SSE tabs
  - `activeRequestSection` (`RequestSectionTab`) — Current request-side editor section; defaults vary by protocol
  - `activeResponseSection` (`ResponseSectionTab`) — Current response-side section; defaults vary by protocol

- **RealtimeTabState** (new, ephemeral):
  - `tabId` (string) — Foreign key to `TabData.id`
  - `protocol` (`'ws' | 'sse'`) — Realtime protocol for this tab; HTTP tabs never create a realtime state entry
  - `connectionId` (string | null) — Active adapter/service connection handle ID
  - `status` (`ConnectionStatus`) — `'idle' | 'connecting' | 'connected' | 'disconnecting' | 'disconnected' | 'error'`
  - `responseHeaders` (`Record<string, string>`) — Handshake or initial response headers captured after connect
  - `wsMessages` (`WsMessage[]`) — Ordered WS message timeline; used only when `protocol === 'ws'`
  - `sseEvents` (`SseEvent[]`) — Ordered SSE event timeline; used only when `protocol === 'sse'`
  - `selectedSseEventName` (`'all' | string`) — Current SSE event-name filter; defaults to `'all'`
  - `error` (string | undefined) — Latest connection or stream error
  - `connectedAt` (number | undefined) — Timestamp of most recent successful connect

- **WsMessage** (new):
  - `id` (string) — Stable message ID
  - `direction` (`'sent' | 'received'`) — Message direction
  - `content` (string) — Text payload for the MVP
  - `timestamp` (number) — Arrival/send time in epoch milliseconds
  - `size` (number) — Payload size in bytes

- **SseEvent** (new):
  - `id` (string) — Stable event row ID
  - `eventName` (string) — SSE event type; defaults to `'message'`
  - `data` (string) — Event payload text
  - `eventId` (string | undefined) — Optional SSE `id:` field
  - `timestamp` (number) — Event time in epoch milliseconds

- **RealtimeStateByTabId** (new):
  - `Record<string, RealtimeTabState>` — Zustand-owned lookup keyed by `tabId`

### Relationships

- `TabData` 1:1 `AnyCollectionRequest` through `loadRequestIntoTab()` and `getCollectionRequest()`.
- `TabData` 0:1 `RealtimeTabState` via `RealtimeTabState.tabId`; only WS/SSE tabs have an entry.
- `RealtimeTabState` 1:N `WsMessage` via `wsMessages` when `protocol === 'ws'`.
- `RealtimeTabState` 1:N `SseEvent` via `sseEvents` when `protocol === 'sse'`.

### Indexes & Constraints

- `RealtimeStateByTabId` keys must be unique per `tabId`.
- HTTP tabs must not retain a `RealtimeTabState` entry.
- `wsMessages` and `sseEvents` are mutually exclusive by protocol; the unused array stays empty.
- `selectedSseEventName` is valid only for SSE tabs and must reset to `'all'` when realtime state is reset.
- Realtime state is in-memory only and must be removed when the tab is cleared or closed.

## 3. Files

- `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts` — Realtime domain types, defaults, and store-facing helpers.
- `packages/core/src/types/tab.ts` — Tighten `TabData.protocol` to `RequestProtocol` and add protocol-aware section/default helpers.
- `packages/core/src/hooks/store/createRealtimeSlice.tsx` — Zustand slice for per-tab realtime connection state and timeline actions.
- `packages/core/src/hooks/store/createRequestTabsSlice.tsx` — Load/save protocol-aware tab drafts and synchronize tab lifecycle with realtime cleanup.
- `packages/core/src/hooks/store/useAppStateStore.tsx` — Compose the new realtime slice into the app store.
- `packages/core/src/index.ts` — Export realtime types/helpers for later FEAT-003, FEAT-006, and FEAT-007 consumers.
- `packages/core/src/test/hooks/store/createRequestTabsSlice.test.ts` — Unit tests for protocol-aware tab serialization and lifecycle behavior.
- `packages/core/src/test/hooks/store/createRealtimeSlice.test.ts` — Unit tests for realtime state transitions, timelines, and filtering.

## 4. Dependencies

- FEAT-001: Protocol Types & Core Data Model must already be complete so `AnyCollectionRequest` and request type guards are available.

## 5. Implementation Tasks

### TASK-001: Open HTTP, WS, and SSE requests into a protocol-aware tab draft

- [x] **Implementation**: Update `packages/core/src/types/tab.ts` and `packages/core/src/hooks/store/createRequestTabsSlice.tsx` so tab state accepts `AnyCollectionRequest` end to end. Tighten `TabData.protocol` to `RequestProtocol`, add protocol-aware request/response section defaults, load legacy HTTP requests with an effective `'http'` protocol, and make `getCollectionRequest()` serialize the correct union member: HTTP keeps method/body/validation, WS omits method/body/validation, SSE keeps method/body but omits validation.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Cover loading a legacy HTTP request with no `protocol`, loading explicit WS and SSE requests into a tab, serializing each tab back to the correct request union, and ensuring WS serialization never leaks HTTP-only fields.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add JSDoc to `TabData`, protocol default helpers, and the request load/save helpers clarifying how each protocol is represented in tab state.
- **Verify**: Calling `loadRequestIntoTab()` followed by `getCollectionRequest()` returns the expected HTTP, WS, or SSE request shape for the same tab.

### TASK-002: Give each WS/SSE tab an isolated idle realtime state entry

- [x] **Implementation**: Create `packages/core/src/types/realtime.ts` and `packages/core/src/hooks/store/createRealtimeSlice.tsx`. Add `RealtimeStateByTabId`, `RealtimeTabState`, `ConnectionStatus`, `WsMessage`, and `SseEvent`, plus slice actions/selectors such as `ensureRealtimeTabState`, `getRealtimeState`, `setRealtimeStatus`, `setRealtimeConnectionId`, `setRealtimeResponseHeaders`, `setRealtimeError`, `resetRealtimeTabState`, and `removeRealtimeTabState`. Compose the slice in `useAppStateStore.tsx` and initialize idle realtime state only for WS/SSE tabs.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Verify a new WS tab gets an idle state, a new SSE tab gets an idle state with `selectedSseEventName === 'all'`, HTTP tabs do not create a realtime entry, and updates for one tab do not mutate another tab's realtime state.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add JSDoc documenting the invariant that realtime state is keyed by tab ID, is ephemeral, and exists only for realtime protocols.
- **Verify**: After creating/loading WS or SSE tabs, `getRealtimeState(tabId)` returns a clean idle state; the same call for an HTTP tab returns no realtime entry.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001

### TASK-003: Track WS messages and SSE events independently per tab

- [x] **Implementation**: Extend `createRealtimeSlice.tsx` with timeline actions and selectors: `appendWsMessage`, `appendSseEvent`, `clearRealtimeTimeline`, `setSseEventFilter`, and `getFilteredSseEvents`. Enforce protocol guards so WS tabs only accept WS messages and SSE tabs only accept SSE events. Preserve insertion order and keep header/error/status fields separate from the timeline arrays.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Cover appending sent/received WS messages in order, appending named and unnamed SSE events, filtering SSE events by event name, clearing only the target tab's timeline, and safe no-op behavior when a message/event is routed to the wrong protocol.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Document the message/event append semantics and the SSE filter contract with short examples in type or slice JSDoc.
- **Verify**: Appending realtime items to one tab only changes that tab's timeline, and `getFilteredSseEvents(tabId)` returns the expected filtered subset.
- **Depends on**: TASK-002

### TASK-004: Reset stale realtime state when a tab changes protocol or lifecycle state

- [x] **Implementation**: Wire `createRequestTabsSlice.tsx` to the realtime slice so `loadRequestIntoTab`, `loadRequestIntoNewTab`, `updateProtocol`, `clearTab`, `clearActiveTab`, and `closeTab` remove or reinitialize realtime state appropriately. Switching from HTTP to WS/SSE should create a fresh idle realtime entry and clear stale `responseData`; switching from WS/SSE back to HTTP should remove realtime state entirely. Reloading a request into an existing tab should discard old messages/events/headers/error state instead of reusing them.
- [x] **Unit Tests**: Cover HTTP → WS initialization, WS → HTTP cleanup, WS ↔ SSE reset semantics, clearing a tab, closing a tab, and reloading the same request into an existing tab with stale realtime state present.
- [ ] **Documentation Update**: Add inline documentation on lifecycle cleanup rules and the “no persistence for realtime state” requirement where tab and realtime slices interact.
- **Verify**: After protocol switches, clears, or tab close, no orphan realtime entries remain in `RealtimeStateByTabId`, and reopened/reloaded tabs always start from clean realtime state.
- **Depends on**: TASK-001, TASK-002, TASK-003

## 6. Cross-Cutting Concerns

- **Auth**: The store continues to hold only `authId` references on `TabData`; no auth credentials, headers, or resolved tokens are copied into realtime state.
- **Error Handling**: Realtime slice actions must be no-throw and deterministic. Unknown tab IDs or protocol-mismatched message/event updates should no-op safely. Connection failures are represented as `status: 'error'` plus a string `error` field, with no adapter I/O performed in this feature.
- **Logging**: This feature should avoid logging message/event payloads from the core store. If debug logs are added for state transitions, log only tab ID, protocol, connection ID, and aggregate counts; never log auth headers, tokens, or full payload bodies.

## 7. State Tracking

- [x] TASK-001: Open HTTP, WS, and SSE requests into a protocol-aware tab draft
- [x] TASK-002: Give each WS/SSE tab an isolated idle realtime state entry
- [x] TASK-003: Track WS messages and SSE events independently per tab
- [x] TASK-004: Reset stale realtime state when a tab changes protocol or lifecycle state
